name: Swordfish Marinated in Lemon, Rosemary and Garlic
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon snipped fresh rosemary, or 1 teaspoon dried rosemary leaves
1 garlic clove, crushed through a press
salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the lemon zest, oil, rosemary, and garlic in a large, shallow platter or tray large enough to hold the swordfish steaks in a single layer.
2. Add the swordfish steaks, sprinkle with salt and pepper, and turn to coat with the marinade.
3. Cover and marinate in the refrigerator for 1 to 2 hours.
4. Brush the grill or broiler pan with a thin film of oil. Preheat the grill or broiler just before serving time.
5. Transfer the swordfish to the grill or broiler pan, reserving the marinade on the platter. If broiling, adjust the oven rack so that the tops of the fish are about 3 inches from the heat source.
6. Grill or broil until lightly browned and opaque when tested with the tip of a knife, about 5 to 8 minutes, depending on the intensity of the heat.
7. Using a spatula, carefully turn each steak. Spoon any remaining marinade and pieces of lemon zest and rosemary onto the tops of the steaks.
8. Broil until lightly browned, about 5 to 8 minutes. Serve garnished with capers and lemon wedges.
Yield: 4 servings
